text.title: product-lifecycle Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-lifecycle as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Applying growth acquisition tactics to a Decline-stage product.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Acquisition spend compounds losses — acquired customers still generate support cost during the sunset window with no long-term LTV payback.

failure-mode.description: Refusing to declare Decline because the label is politically costly — lobbying for "late Maturity" indefinitely.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Delayed Decline classification extends over-investment; pre-commit numeric kill thresholds (e.g., "MRR YoY < 0 for 3 quarters = automatic Decline review") to remove the political judgment call.

failure-mode.description: Using revenue YoY growth as the sole classification signal for network-effect or bundling products.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Network-effect products can show low revenue but high engagement (still Growth); bundling products can show stable revenue while the real value is migrating to a different SKU.

failure-mode.description: Same metrics tracked regardless of lifecycle stage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Growth rate is the primary metric in Introduction and Growth but meaningless in Maturity (where profitability and churn matter more) and actively misleading in Decline.

failure-mode.description: Emotional attachment to a declining product preventing objective sunset decision.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Emotional attachment delays the inevitable, extends over-investment, and wastes capacity that could accelerate a Growth-stage product. Pre-committed numeric thresholds are the antidote.

failure-mode.description: No sunset plan drafted until Decline is obvious.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sunset planning requires 6-12 months of lead time for customer communication, contract wind-down, and team transition. Waiting until Decline is confirmed leaves insufficient runway.
